title: Air-gapped identity with mTLS and short-lived credentials
description: Provides service identity and authentication in air-gapped environments by issuing short-lived X.509 certificates from an internal PKI and enforcing mutual TLS between services. Replaces external OIDC/OAuth2 providers that may be unreachable in isolated network segments.

cost.costNotes: May be near-$0 incremental cost if org already runs internal PKI.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Min $0 assumes organization already operates internal PKI infrastructure (Vault, EJBCA, or similar) with existing capacity. Max $800 covers dedicated PKI infrastructure for air-gapped environment: self-hosted HashiCorp Vault on 2-3 VMs ($200-300 for compute/storage), internal certificate authority operations ($100-200 for automation/monitoring), and cert-manager or similar automation tooling ($0-300 for commercial support). Air-gapped environments typically use existing hardware, so incremental cost is primarily for dedicated PKI services if not already present. Service mesh components (Istio, Linkerd) are open-source with $0 direct cost but may require additional compute resources.
provenance.adoptionCost: High complexity pattern requiring: (1) PKI infrastructure design and deployment (10-15 hours) including root CA, intermediate CAs, and certificate policies for air-gapped constraints, (2) mTLS integration across service mesh or application layer (15-20 hours) with proper certificate validation, (3) short-lived credential rotation automation (10-15 hours) including cert-manager, renewal webhooks, and graceful rotation logic, (4) operational runbooks and disaster recovery procedures critical for air-gapped environments (8-10 hours), (5) security policy definition and least-privilege access controls (5-8 hours). Total 48-68 hours at $75/hour blended rate with AI assistance = $3,600-5,100. Medium-high estimate of $3,500 accounts for AI-assisted implementation reducing custom scripting but substantial testing required for air-gapped reliability.
provenance.licenseCost: Open-source PKI solutions available: HashiCorp Vault (MPL 2.0), cert-manager (Apache 2.0), EJBCA Community (LGPL), OpenSSL (Apache-style), CFSSL (BSD). Air-gapped environments typically mandate open-source or internally auditable solutions due to compliance requirements. Commercial PKI solutions (Venafi, Entrust) range $20,000-100,000+ annually but are unnecessary for this pattern. Assumes open-source tooling with internal support rather than commercial enterprise PKI platforms.

provides.reasoning: This pattern explicitly provides mutual TLS authentication between services in air-gapped environments, which is a form of service-to-service authentication.
The pattern specifically mentions short-lived cert rotation as a core feature, directly providing automated credential lifecycle management.
This pattern inherently provides certificate lifecycle management as part of the short-lived cert rotation capability, including issuance, renewal, and revocation.
mTLS with short-lived certs and no external dependencies aligns with zero-trust principles by requiring continuous verification and assuming no implicit trust.

requires.reasoning: Internal PKI (Public Key Infrastructure) is explicitly mentioned as necessary for issuing and managing the certificates used in mTLS authentication.
The pattern requires an active Certificate Authority for issuing and signing certificates as part of the internal PKI infrastructure.
The pattern depends on mutual TLS protocol implementation capabilities to establish authenticated and encrypted bidirectional connections between services.

supports_nfr.reason: Pattern supports availability up to 99.99% with internal PKI infrastructure and certificate validation overhead.
mTLS handshake and certificate validation add minimum 10ms overhead at p95.
Certificate validation with CRL/OCSP checks can add up to 50ms at p99.
Pattern supports RTO >= 1 minute with internal PKI failover and local certificate authority recovery.
Pattern explicitly implements mTLS authentication with internal PKI for air-gapped environments.
Pattern requires audit logging for certificate issuance, rotation, and authentication events.
Pattern provides strong authentication suitable for HIPAA but does not guarantee full compliance.
Pattern secures authentication layer independent of CCPA data privacy requirements.
mTLS with audit logging supports SOX authentication requirements but does not enforce full compliance.

supports_constraints.reason: Pattern specifically designed for air-gapped on-premise environments where external OIDC providers are unavailable.
This is an infrastructure pattern for mTLS-based identity applicable to all programming languages.
Identity and authentication pattern applies to all platform types requiring service-to-service communication.

pki_type.description: PKI architecture: internal-ca for single root CA (simpler), intermediate-ca for hierarchical trust (more scalable), distributed-ca for multi-site air-gapped environments

cert_lifetime_hours.description: Certificate validity period in hours. Shorter lifetimes (1-8h) increase security but require more frequent rotation; longer lifetimes (72-168h) reduce operational overhead but increase blast radius if compromised

rotation_strategy.description: Certificate rotation approach: automated for zero-touch renewal (requires agent), semi-automated for approval-based renewal (human-in-loop), manual for high-security environments with change control

cert_storage.description: Private key storage: filesystem for simplicity, hsm for hardware-backed security, secrets-manager for centralized vault, memory-only for ephemeral workloads

revocation_method.description: Certificate revocation: crl for offline distribution lists, ocsp for online status checking (requires responder), short-lived-only to avoid revocation infrastructure by relying on expiration
